entries.statement: ∀ {α : Type _} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α] [ACR.C5 α], Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) ↔ (⊠(⊤ : α)) ≐ (⊠⊠(⊤ : α))
entries.docstring_summary: Gödel fixpoints exist if and only if the refutability operator is idempotent on top (under the current ACR/APS/C5 assumptions).
entries.rationale: This is the clean structural characterization organizing the current cluster: existing results already show the forward implication (existence forces ⊠⊤ ≐ ⊠⊠⊤), and the reverse implication is a canonical construction (take g := ⊠⊤).
entries.plan_summary: Prove the ↔ by splitting into two implications. For the forward direction, reuse the derived theorem `thm_reft_top_equiv_double_reft_top_000008`, which already states that `Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α)` implies `⊠⊤ ≐ ⊠⊠⊤` under the same typeclass assumptions. For the reverse direction, construct an explicit Gödel fixpoint by taking `g := ⊠(⊤ : α)` and use the assumed equivalence `⊠⊤ ≐ ⊠⊠⊤` to discharge the subtype condition `g ≐ ⊠g`.
intermediate_lemmas: If h : (⊠(⊤ : α)) ≐ (⊠⊠(⊤ : α)), then Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) by taking the witness g with g.1 = ⊠(⊤ : α) and g.2 = h (after unfolding ⊠⊠).
Unfolding/rewriting helper: (⊠(⊠(⊤ : α))) definitional-equals (⊠⊠(⊤ : α)), so h can be used directly as a proof of (⊠(⊤ : α)) ≐ ⊠(⊠(⊤ : α)).
